zh.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède是Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède的兒子，於大約0940年出生在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利坦卡維爾。他和Helissende de Abbetot至少有6個兒子。他去世於1041年在他的家鄉享年102歲，埋葬在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利沙吉馬-德-波黑維爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède是Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède的儿子，于大约0940年出生在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利坦卡維爾。他和Helissende de Abbetot至少有6个儿子。他去世于1041年在他的家乡享年102岁，埋葬在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利沙吉馬-德-波黑維爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Ville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1 English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Déville in Seine-Maritime, France, probably named with Latin dei villa ‘settlement of (i.e. under the protection of) God’. This name was interpreted early on as a prepositional phrase de ville or de val and applied to dwellers in a town or valley ( see Ville and Vale ).

2 English: nickname from Middle English devyle, Old English dēofol ‘devil’ (Latin diabolus, from Greek diabolos ‘slanderer’, ‘enemy’), referring to a mischievous youth or perhaps to someone who had acted the role of the Devil in a pageant or mystery play.
3 French: variant of Ville , with the preposition de.

variants.name: Devall
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Déville in Seine-Maritime. The full form was de Daiville but when the preposition was omitted, Daiville was taken to be for de Aiville.
English: alternatively the name may be from Middle English devel (Old English dēofol) ‘devil, Satan’, possibly acquired as a pageant name or as a shortened form of a longer nickname; compare Deeble .
Altered form of French   Duval   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Duvall  .

variants.name: Vale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Abilio, Angel, Fernando, Jose Joel, Miguel, Pedro, Roberto, Adriana, Alberto, Alfredo.

English: of Norman origin, a topographic name for someone who lived ‘(in the) vale or valley’ (Middle English val(e), valle, Old French val ‘valley’), or a habitational name for someone who came from one of the French places called with this word, such as Le Val (Sarthe) or any number of places in and around Normandy called Vaux. Compare Vause .
Irish: from Mac Pháil ‘son of Paul’.

nameMeanings.name: Tancrède
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Rocco, Salvatore, Silvio, Aniello, Carmino, Gaetano, Giovanni, Guido, Italo, Luigi, Pasquale.
Italian: from the personal name Tancredi, composed of the ancient Germanic elements thank ‘thought’ + rēd ‘counsel, advice’.

variants.name: Tranchina
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Salvatore, Angelo, Luigi, Sebastiano.
Italian (Sicily): possibly from the personal name Tranchida, derived from Tancrida, feminine form of the personal name Tancredi .

variants.name: Tango
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Domenic, Gioia, Raffaela, Rocco, Vito.

Italian: probably from a personal name of ancient Germanic origin composed with the element thank ‘thought’ (see Tancredi ); alternatively, perhaps in some cases, from the Italian Greek name Tangos, unexplained.

variants.name: Tangredi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Salvatore, Amedeo, Aniello, Dino.
Italian (Campania): variant of   Tancredi  .

nameMeanings.name: Geraldus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Gerald , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Gerald
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + wald ‘rule’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. There has been some confusion with Gerard . It died out in England at the end of the 13th century. However, it continued to be popular in Ireland, where it had been brought in the 12th century at the time of Strongbow's invasion. It was used in England in the 17th century and revived in the 19th century, along with several other long-extinct names of Norman, Old English, and Celtic origin, and is now more common than Gerard, which survived all along as an English ‘gentry’ name.

variants.name: Jeralds
meanings: Variant of   Geralds  .

variants.name: Gearlds
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Geralds  .

en.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède was born about 0940, in Tancarville, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France as the son of Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède. He had at least 6 sons with Helissende de Abbetot. He died in 1041, in his hometown, at the age of 102, and was buried in Saint-Martin-de-Boscherville, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède est né environ 0940, à Tancarville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, fils de Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède. Il a eu au moins 6 fils avec Helissende de Abbetot. Il est décédé en 1041, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 102 ans, et a été enterré à Saint-Martin-de-Boscherville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède wurde um 0940 in Tancarville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich, als Sohn von Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède geboren. Er hatte mit Helissende de Abbetot mindestens 6 Söhne. Er starb 1041 im Alter von 102 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Saint-Martin-de-Boscherville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède è nato il circa 0940 nel Tancarville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia come figlio di Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli con Helissende de Abbetot. Morì nel 1041, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 102 anni, e fu sepolto a Saint-Martin-de-Boscherville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrèdeは約0940年にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメタンカーヴィルでSeigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrèdeの息子として生まれた。彼はHelissende de Abbetotとの間に少なくとも6人の息子をもうけた。彼は1041年に故郷で102歳で死去し，フランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメサイント-マルティン-デ-ボシェルヴィルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède(은)는 대략 0940년 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 탠카빌에서 Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Helissende de Abbetot(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1041년 그의 고향에서 102세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 세인트-마틴-데-보슈르빌에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède nasceu aproximadamente 0940, em Tancarville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França filho de Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos com Helissende de Abbetot. Ele faleceu em 1041, em sua cidade natal, com 102 anos, e foi sepultado em Saint-Martin-de-Boscherville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède родился около 0940 г. в местности танкарвилл, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция. Его отца звали Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей от Helissende de Abbetot. Он умер в 1041 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 102 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинт-мартин-де-бощирвилл, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Geraldus I de la Ville Tancrède nació aproximadamente 0940, en Tancarville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia como hijo de Seigneur Rabel, Second de la Ville Tancrède. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos con Helissende de Abbetot. Murió en 1041, en su pueblo, a la edad de 102 años, y fue sepultado en Saint-Martin-de-Boscherville, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia.
